title: Reliable Bash Automation and Tests · Operations Defense
summary: Turn a fragile command history into a bounded script that validates inputs, preserves data, reports failures, and can be rerun safely. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a tested idempotent Bash operations script. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
Use set options, traps, explicit conditionals, and status handling while understanding where errexit and pipefail do not behave intuitively.
Handle positional parameters, defaults, validation, arrays, and quoted expansion without word splitting or glob injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Script Contract · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A report script receives filenames "north 5.csv" and "--dry-run" plus one missing file. Preserve argument boundaries, validate before mutation, clean temporary state, and prove rerunning is idempotent.

Which action demonstrates Script Contract without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Script Contract evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Script Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.
